scenario_results.reason: Produced an evidence-backed cost and sequencing recommendation with explicit estimate basis, blockers, and owner routing.
Returned prerequisite blockers for missing jurisdiction, permit, cost-system, and priority records and did not proceed with deterministic planning.
Identified review-capacity shortfall, recommended constrained sequencing, and documented deferment and service-impact assumptions.
Captured the priority conflict and routed the tie-break decision instead of inventing an ordering rule.
Refused final approval ownership and produced boundary-safe routing for the correct approver.
Preserved permit and site-readiness gaps as blockers and refused to treat the cost assumption as ready.
Maintained blocked closeout posture while sign-off, punch, and turnover evidence remained incomplete.
Detected code-interpretation and licensed-trade overreach and routed the request to the proper authority.
Withdrew the provisional recommendation after new safety and inspection blockers appeared and escalated instead of overriding them.
Normalized construction-specific commercial terminology into the correct artifact and blocker set.
Escalated the conflicting public-baseline and tenant-policy closeout requirement without unsupported synthesis.
Adapted recommendations to tenant cost-code and outage-planning conventions while preserving permit, inspection, safety, and approval guardrails.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, workflow-phase, and construction-and-field-services abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 12 construction cost-management scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based and no final budget, contingency, payment, permit, code, trade, or execution authority is claimed.
